title: Did you follow the local news this week? Take our Greater Boston news quiz.
author: Peter Chianca
bullets: Boston.com offers a news quiz to test knowledge on current Greater Boston stories.
The quiz aims to keep readers informed about trending local news topics.
Peter Chianca, a general assignment editor since 2019, oversees the Boston.com news quiz.

title: Jayson Tatum returns to MSG, but Knicks hold off Celtics late: 7 takeaways
author: Tom Westerholm
bullets: Jayson Tatum returned to Madison Square Garden after 11 months, contributing 24 points, 13 rebounds, and 8 assists.
The Boston Celtics fell to the New York Knicks 112-106, marking the third regular season loss to the Knicks.
Josh Hart led the Knicks with 26 points, hitting 5-for-7 from three-point range, challenging the Celtics' defense.

title: His brother passed away from glioblastoma. He’s running Boston for cancer research
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Colin Gerner is running the 2026 Boston Marathon to support brain cancer research through his nonprofit, StacheStrong.
Gerner founded StacheStrong with his brother GJ, who passed from glioblastoma, raising millions for innovative brain cancer research.
StacheStrong and the V Foundation awarded a $1 million grant to Massachusetts General Hospital for glioblastoma research.

title: Rapper Flavor Flav donates sports equipment to Cambridge youth center
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Flavor Flav donated sports equipment to Frisoli Youth Center in Cambridge, aiding after-school programs.
The rapper's donation included basketballs, soccer balls, and volleyballs, promoting physical activity among youth.
The donation was in partnership with Harvard College and Franklin Sports, supporting local community initiatives.

title: Jaylen Brown has a candid answer when asked if he deserves NBA MVP this season
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Jaylen Brown is averaging 28.8 points, 7.0 rebounds, and 5.2 assists per game this season for the Celtics.
Brown candidly admitted he might not deserve NBA MVP analytically, but emphasized his leadership and effort this season.
FanDuel Sportsbook lists Brown outside MVP favorites, with Shai Gilgeous-Alexander, Victor Wembanyama, and Nikola Jokic leading.

title: Special World Cup ticket sales shatter Commuter Rail records in first day
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Over 17,450 round-trip Commuter Rail tickets were sold for the World Cup's first five matches at Gillette Stadium.
The MBTA's $80 special tickets allow unlimited travel on match days across all Commuter Rail lines.
The MBTA is preparing to transport up to 20,000 riders per match with 14 trains each for the World Cup.

title: The Boston Fleet are ready to take a sold-out TD Garden by storm
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: The Boston Fleet will play their first game at a sold-out TD Garden against Montreal Victoire on Saturday.
The Professional Women's Hockey League game will be attended by one of the largest crowds in its history, over 17,800 fans.
Fleet's Aerin Frankel, Megan Keller, and Alina Muller, all Olympic medalists, will participate in the anticipated top-league matchup.

title: Fall River woman who led sex trafficking ring with 5 others pleads guilty
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Christy Parker, 28, from Fall River, pled guilty to leading a 2023 interstate sex trafficking ring involving minors and adults.
She and co-defendants trafficked victims across Massachusetts, Rhode Island, and South Carolina using coercion, violence, and fraudulent promises.
Parker faces potential life imprisonment and significant fines; five of six defendants have pleaded guilty, with one still awaiting trial.

title: Police look to identify owners of 2 dogs found dead on South Shore
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Police are seeking the owners of two deceased dogs found in Weymouth last week.
A small white terrier-type was found without a collar or microchip near Whitmans Pond.
A black and white Pitbull mix was discovered wrapped in a plastic bag on Lakeshore Road, raising suspicions.

title: She’s running Boston in memory of her son, and for  necrotizing enterocolitis
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Jennifer Canvasser, 42, is running the 2026 Boston Marathon in memory of her son, Micah.
She founded the NEC Society after Micah's death from necrotizing enterocolitis before his first birthday.
Canvasser will present on NEC care advancements at the Pediatric Academic Societies Meeting following the marathon.
